_meta.reason: Bypass ggml-metal kernel crash and ollama-httpx timeout walls on dense equipment-code content. Free under existing subscription.

chunks.content:
# Operations — Production & Logistics

## L1 Philosophy: A Factory, Not a Restaurant

The L1 Kitchen operates on the Central Kitchen model. We do NOT cook to order for guests. We produce batches of food that are then cooled, packaged, and shipped to the sales point (L2).

## The Main Algorithm (Cook-Chill)

1. Receiving & Storage (Logistics) — Zone 1
2. Prep & Cutting (Cold Prep) — Zone 2
3. Thermal Processing (Hot Production) — Zone 3
4. Shock Cooling (Blast Chilling) — Critical Step — Zone 4
5. Vacuum Sealing (Packaging) — Zone 4
6. Dispatch (Transport) — to L2
7. Heat in high-speed oven Merrychef — at L2

## Kitchen Zones

### Zone 1: Logistics & Storage
Products arrive from suppliers. The warehouse inside the kitchen.

Dry Storage & Inventory Racks: L1-SHELF-200-54 (Central Pantry), L1-SHELF-200-55 (Logistics Hub — vacuum bags, HACCP labels, containers).

Main Cold Storage (The Warehouse): L1-SPM-FRG-200-25 — Large 2-door supermarket fridge (1200L). Zone L1-Store-W3. Buffer zone for incoming raw materials.

### Zone 2: Cold Prep (Clean Label)
Washing, peeling, cutting. Where prep is born.

Washing & Water Safety (The Kidney of the Kitchen): L1-DBL-SINK-180-5 (Double sink) + L1-WAT-HF-UV-69 (UV Water Filter). Zone L1-Cold-W2. UV lamp is CRITICAL — Phuket water is unsafe for salads and drinking.

Grease Trap: L1-GR-TRAP-38 — under the sink. Must be cleaned manually if water drains slowly.

Prep Workstation (Mise-en-place): L1-UC-FRG-180-3 (Under-counter Fridge). Zone L1-Cold-W1. Surface = main cutting table. Inside = washed/peeled vegetables (+2C...+5C).

Veg Slicer L1-VEG-SLCR-CHINA-12: Mass cutting (slices, cubes) for high volumes.
Bowl Cutter L1-BL-CUT-8L-19: 8-liter Robot for hummus, pesto, forcemeat. Sits on rubber mat for vibration.

Dough Mixer (Heavy Artillery): L1-D-MIX-10KG-18 — Spiral mixer for 10kg dough. Zone L1-Cold-Corner. On special low stand L1-SS-STND-50-72 (50cm height) for ergonomic unloading.

### Zone 3: Hot Production
Where flavor magic happens (Maillard reaction). Under powerful ventilation.

Extraction Hood (The Lungs of the Kitchen): L1-KTC-HOOD-150-31. Without it, grill operation is impossible.

Frying & Boiling Line on reinforced stand L1-SS-STND-160-68 (38cm + wheels):
Gas Range 4-Burner L1-GAS-RNG-570-32: The Workhorse — soups, stocks, quinoa.
Lava Grill L1-LAVA-GRILL-650-33: Volcanic rocks, fat drips → smoke → BBQ flavor. Proteins (chicken, tofu, halloumi) without excess oil.

Baking Station: L1-EL-CON-OVEN-83-20 — Convection Oven (3 levels). Zone L1-Hot-W3. On stand L1-SS-STND-120-71 with tray runners. For roasting root vegetables and baking croissants.

### Zone 4: Cook-Chill Hub (Heart of Technology)
The most important stage. Turns food into safe, long-storage semi-finished products.

Blast Chiller (The Time Machine): L1-BL-FRZ-790-66 — CRITICAL UNIT.
Algorithm: Chef removes hot tray (+90C) → IMMEDIATELY into Blast Chiller → +3C in <90 minutes.
Why: Bacteria can't multiply, moisture stays locked in meat fibers. Foundation of food safety.
BOTTLENECK: If Blast Chiller is full, no new hot cooking batch can start. Logistics L1→L2 only possible after cycle: Cook → Shock → Vacuum → Label.

Vacuum Sealer: L1-VAC-500-67 — Chamber Vacuum Sealer. On table in Hot Zone (L1-Hot-W2).
Algorithm: Chilled product portioned → bag → machine removes 99.9% of air.
Result: Shelf life 7-10 days. No oxidation, no taste degradation.

Assembly Station (The Pass): L1-UC-FRZ-180-22 — Island Freezer (Under-counter). Center of Hot Zone L1-Hot-W1. Chef's assembly table. Sauces and microgreens stored inside.

### Auxiliary Units
Blender L1-KITCH-BLND-CHINA-13: On table in cold zone. For hot puree soups and sauces. Powerful, noisy.
Juicer L1-JUIC-EXTR-CHINA-15: For detox drink bases and marinades (ginger/celery juice). Requires thorough mesh cleaning immediately after use.

## Daily Operations Model
- L1 preps batches: morning 07:00-10:00 — 10-12kg toppings, bottled sauces, dough.
- Delivery to L2: motorbike, 5 min, 3x/day.
- Split: 80% take-away, 20% eat-in.

## Staffing
- 4-6 people total
- An (cashier/admin)
- 2 Burmese prep staff (interviews pending)
- Prep-chef or consultant for recipes — one-time
- Training: 1 week on checklists/HACCP

entities.description: The first physical Shishka Healthy Kitchen — a central production kitchen (factory model), NOT a restaurant. Produces batches of food for downstream sales point L2. Operates on the Cook-Chill algorithm. 4-6 staff.

Sales point, downstream of L1 central kitchen. Receives chilled vacuum-sealed semi-finished products from L1 by motorbike (3x daily, 5 min trip). Final reheat happens here in Merrychef high-speed oven. Split: 80 percent take-away, 20 percent eat-in.
Production philosophy: batch cooking ahead, cooled, packaged, shipped to sales point. Opposite of restaurant model where dishes are cooked to order. Core to L1 operations.

Seven-step production process: 1) Receiving & Storage (Zone 1), 2) Prep & Cutting (Zone 2), 3) Thermal Processing (Zone 3), 4) Shock Cooling/Blast Chilling (Zone 4, critical step), 5) Vacuum Sealing (Zone 4), 6) Dispatch to L2, 7) Reheat in Merrychef at L2. Foundation of food safety and shelf life.
High-speed oven located at L2 sales point. Final reheating step in the Cook-Chill algorithm. Brings vacuum-sealed chilled product to serving temperature quickly.

Receiving and storage zone in L1 Kitchen. The 'warehouse inside the kitchen'. Contains main cold storage, dry storage shelves, logistics hub for vacuum bags, HACCP labels, containers. Buffer zone for incoming raw materials from suppliers.

Clean label zone for washing, peeling, cutting in L1 Kitchen. 'Where prep is born'. Contains double sink with UV water filter (kidney of kitchen), prep fridge, vegetable slicer, bowl cutter, dough mixer. Critical for food safety due to Phuket water issues.
Cooking zone in L1 Kitchen where Maillard reaction happens. Under powerful extraction hood ventilation. Contains gas range, lava grill, convection oven. The flavor magic stage.

Heart of technology in L1 Kitchen. The most important stage of production. Turns hot cooked food into safe long-storage semi-finished products. Contains blast chiller (critical), vacuum sealer, assembly station. Bottleneck zone for the entire L1 throughput.
Main cold storage in Zone 1. Large 2-door supermarket fridge, 1200 liters capacity. Located in L1-Store-W3. Buffer zone for incoming raw materials. Called The Warehouse.

UV Water Filter in Zone 2 cold prep. CRITICAL piece of equipment because Phuket tap water is unsafe for salads and drinking. Part of the 'kidney of the kitchen' water safety system together with the double sink. Without it, raw vegetable prep is impossible.
Double sink in Zone 2 cold prep. Together with UV water filter forms the 'kidney of the kitchen' water safety system.
Under-counter prep fridge in Zone 2 (L1-Cold-W1). Surface serves as main cutting table. Interior holds washed/peeled vegetables at +2C to +5C.
Vegetable slicer for mass cutting (slices, cubes) at high volumes in Zone 2 cold prep.
8-liter bowl cutter (called The Robot) in Zone 2. Used for hummus, pesto, forcemeat. Sits on rubber mat to absorb vibration.
Spiral dough mixer with 10kg capacity (Heavy Artillery) in Zone 2 corner. On low ergonomic stand L1-SS-STND-50-72 (50cm height) for unloading.
Kitchen extraction hood in Zone 3 hot production. Called The Lungs of the Kitchen. Without it, grill operation is impossible due to smoke.
4-burner gas range called The Workhorse in Zone 3. Used for soups, stocks, quinoa.
Lava grill with volcanic rocks in Zone 3 hot production. Fat drips create smoke for BBQ flavor. Cooks proteins (chicken, tofu, halloumi) without excess oil.
Electric convection oven with 3 levels in Zone 3 baking station (L1-Hot-W3). For roasting root vegetables and baking croissants. On stand with tray runners.

Blast chiller in Zone 4. Called The Time Machine. CRITICAL UNIT and BOTTLENECK of entire L1 throughput. Algorithm: hot tray at +90C goes immediately into the blast chiller and reaches +3C in under 90 minutes. Bacteria cannot multiply, moisture stays locked in meat fibers. Foundation of food safety. If full, no new hot cooking batch can start.

Chamber vacuum sealer in Zone 4 (L1-Hot-W2). Algorithm: chilled product portioned, bagged, 99.9 percent of air removed. Result: shelf life 7-10 days, no oxidation, no taste degradation. Enables Cook-Chill end-to-end.
Island under-counter freezer at Assembly Station (The Pass) in center of Hot Zone L1-Hot-W1. Chef's assembly table. Stores sauces and microgreens inside.

Local tap water in Phuket — UNSAFE for direct use in salads and drinking. The reason UV Water Filter L1-WAT-HF-UV-69 is critical equipment in Zone 2 cold prep. Drives the food safety architecture of L1.
Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points — food safety standard. L1 staff receive 1 week of training on HACCP and operational checklists. HACCP labels stored in Logistics Hub L1-SHELF-200-55.
Cashier and admin staff at L1 Kitchen. Part of the 4-6 person L1 team.
Two Burmese prep staff at L1 Kitchen. Interviews pending as of 2026-04-05. Part of the 4-6 person L1 team.
L1 morning prep window 07:00-10:00 produces 10-12kg of toppings, bottled sauces, and dough per day. Delivery to L2 by motorbike, 5 minutes one way, 3 times per day.

relationships.description: L1 Kitchen operates on the Central Kitchen model — produces batches in advance rather than cooking to order. This is the founding philosophy of L1.
relationships.keywords: model, philosophy, batch production, factory not restaurant

relationships.description: L1 Kitchen executes the seven-step Cook-Chill algorithm as its main production process. All zones serve this algorithm.
relationships.keywords: main process, production workflow, cook chill

relationships.description: L1 produces batches and ships chilled vacuum-sealed product to L2 sales point by motorbike, 5 minutes one way, 3 times daily.
Merrychef high-speed oven is located at L2 and performs the final reheat step of the Cook-Chill algorithm before serving guests.
relationships.keywords: reheat, final step, high speed oven

relationships.description: UV Water Filter is required because Phuket tap water is unsafe for salads and drinking. This is the reason it is classified as critical equipment in Zone 2.
relationships.keywords: water safety, UV, salads, critical, Phuket

relationships.description: Main cold storage L1-SPM-FRG-200-25 (1200L 2-door fridge) is located in Zone 1, serving as buffer for incoming raw materials.
UV Water Filter is part of the water safety system in Zone 2 cold prep — the kidney of the kitchen.
Prep workstation under-counter fridge L1-UC-FRG-180-3 is located in Zone 2. Surface is the main cutting table; interior holds washed vegetables at +2C to +5C.
relationships.keywords: zone 2, prep, fridge, mise en place

relationships.description: Lava grill is located in Zone 3 hot production line, used for grilling proteins like chicken, tofu, halloumi without excess oil.
Extraction hood is mounted in Zone 3 hot production. Without it, the grill cannot operate safely.
Blast chiller is located in Zone 4 — the heart of cook-chill technology. Most critical equipment in the entire kitchen.
relationships.keywords: zone 4, blast chiller, critical, time machine

relationships.description: Vacuum sealer is located in Zone 4 cook-chill hub. Step 5 of the Cook-Chill algorithm.
Blast chiller is THE critical step (step 4) of the Cook-Chill algorithm. Without it, food safety is impossible. Bottleneck — if blast chiller is full, no new hot batch can start.
relationships.keywords: critical step, food safety, bottleneck, blast chiller

relationships.description: Vacuum sealer is step 5 of the Cook-Chill algorithm. Removes 99.9 percent of air, achieving 7-10 day shelf life with no oxidation.
L1 staff receive 1 week of training on HACCP and operational checklists as part of onboarding.
An is the cashier/admin at L1 Kitchen, part of the 4-6 person team.
Two Burmese prep staff are part of the L1 Kitchen 4-6 person team. Interviews pending as of 2026-04-05.
L1 Kitchen runs a daily batch schedule: morning prep window 07:00-10:00, 10-12kg toppings/sauces/dough produced, 3x daily delivery to L2.
